seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient is a female born on 01/05/1986
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: This is a new patient visit for acid reflux
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has a past medical history of anxiety
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has a past medical history of seasonal allergies
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient reports really bad acid reflux
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient currently takes omeprazole 40 mg twice daily for reflux
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Despite omeprazole 40 mg twice daily, some days the patient's reflux symptoms are still really bad
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient describes the reflux sensation as burning
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient feels burning in the chest
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient feels burning in the throat
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies difficulty swallowing or food or liquids getting stuck
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies abdominal pain
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies nausea, vomiting, or changes in bowel movements
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies constipation, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has had acid reflux since around age 12
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's acid reflux has worsened over the past few years
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has been taking omeprazole in the current regimen for about a year
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient feels omeprazole has helped somewhat, with some days symptom-free and other days severe symptoms after eating
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient identifies stress as a trigger that worsens reflux symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient identifies food choices as triggers that worsen reflux symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Spicy foods worsen the patient's reflux symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Soda and carbonated beverages can worsen the patient's reflux symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Tomato-based products sometimes worsen the patient's reflux symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Citrus products sometimes worsen the patient's reflux symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient continues to eat tomato-based and citrus products despite them sometimes worsening symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's weight has been pretty stable
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient reports decreased appetite for the past two months
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has been under a lot of work-related stress
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient works as a paralegal
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's maternal grandmother had esophageal cancer
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's mother has a history of acid reflux
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient may have had an upper endoscopy when younger but not in the last 10 years
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient previously took a combination of omeprazole and ranitidine, which helped
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Aside from omeprazole and ranitidine, the patient has not taken other reflux medications
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Physical exam is normal except for well-healed abdominal surgical scars
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient had abdominal surgery as a baby for twisted intestines at around one month of age
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician assesses that the patient has a several-year history of GERD
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician notes that the patient's GERD symptoms are poorly controlled on omeprazole 40 mg twice daily
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician confirms the patient has heartburn despite taking omeprazole
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician recommends dietary changes to avoid trigger foods such as spicy foods, carbonated beverages, citrus, and tomato-based foods
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician counsels that stress is a trigger that can worsen GERD symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to address stress management to help with GERD
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician proposes switching from omeprazole to Nexium as a different PPI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient agrees to switch to Nexium
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician will prescribe Nexium 40 mg twice daily to the patient's pharmacy
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician
